content: Always include the patient's complete diagnosis history with ICD-10 codes when filling PA forms. Insurance companies frequently reject applications that lack specific diagnostic codes to justify the prescribed treatment.

When a medication has step therapy requirements, document all previous medications tried and the specific reasons they were inadequate (side effects, lack of efficacy with dates and dosages). Generic statements like 'patient failed previous therapy' are insufficient.
Include the prescribing physician's direct phone number and NPI number on every form. Many PA rejections occur simply because the reviewer cannot verify the prescription with the provider.
Blue Cross Blue Shield requires peer-to-peer review documentation for specialty medications over $1000/month. Include a note indicating willingness for peer-to-peer review to expedite the process.

BCBS California processes PA requests faster when the Medi-Cal Rx PA form is used with the supplementary clinical documentation attached as a separate page rather than crammed into the comments section.

Ozempic (semaglutide) PA requests are more successful when accompanied by documentation of: 1) BMI or weight history, 2) A1C levels showing inadequate glycemic control on current medications, 3) Documentation of metformin use for at least 3 months. Most insurers require step therapy documentation.

On the Medi-Cal Rx PA form, the 'Clinical Justification' field (Section 4) is the most critical. Provide specific lab values, dates of previous treatment attempts, and clinical rationale using medical terminology. Vague justifications are the #1 reason for rejection on this form.
